# General Requirements Quality Checklist: Pokedex Page

**Purpose**: Validate the quality, clarity, and completeness of the Pokedex Page specification requirements
**Created**: 2026-05-24
**Feature**: [spec.md](../spec.md)

**Note**: This checklist is generated by the `/speckit.checklist` command based on feature context and requirements.

## Requirement Completeness

- [ ] CHK001 - Is the button's visual treatment (size, color, position on home page) explicitly specified beyond the label "Pokedex"? [Completeness, Spec §FR-001] — *Gap: spec only says "clearly labeled"; tasks (T008) make it concrete by specifying "first pill in the pill-group section."*
- [x] CHK002 - Are the exact data fields to extract from the PokéAPI response documented (e.g., which sprite variant, ID format)? [Completeness, Spec §FR-003] — *Covered in plan.md and tasks.md (T014 specifies ID extraction regex and sprite CDN URL).*
- [ ] CHK003 - Are retry/recovery requirements defined for the error state, given the edge case mentions "option to retry" but no FR covers it? [Gap, Spec §Edge Cases] — *Gap in spec: no explicit FR for retry. Implemented in tasks (T024).*
- [ ] CHK004 - Are the visual properties of the loading indicator (spinner, skeleton, progress bar) specified? [Gap, Spec §FR-005] — *Gap in spec: only says "loading indicator". Tasks (T018) specify text "Loading Pokemon...".*
- [x] CHK005 - Is the "user-friendly error message" content, tone, or structure defined with specific criteria? [Completeness, Spec §FR-006] — *Tasks (T018) specify exact message: "Could not load Pokemon. Please try again."*

## Requirement Clarity

- [ ] CHK006 - Is "prominently" (describing the Pokedex button visibility) quantified with measurable positioning or sizing criteria? [Clarity, Spec §FR-001] — *Gap in spec: "prominently" is subjective. Tasks (T008) make it concrete: "first pill in pill-group."*
- [x] CHK007 - Is the pagination approach explicitly selected between "pagination" and "infinite scroll", or is the ambiguity acceptable? [Ambiguity, Spec §FR-007] — *Resolved: tasks (T022) explicitly choose "Load More" button approach.*
- [x] CHK008 - Is "representative image" defined — specifically which PokéAPI sprite (front_default, official artwork, etc.) should be used? [Clarity, Spec §FR-004] — *Resolved: tasks (T014) specify exact sprite CDN URL template.*
- [x] CHK009 - Is "responsive grid layout" defined with breakpoint behavior, column counts, or minimum card widths? [Ambiguity, Spec §FR-004] — *Resolved: tasks (T018) specify exact breakpoints: 1 col (default), 2 col (sm), 3 col (md), 4 col (lg).* 
- [x] CHK010 - Is "standard broadband connection" quantified with a minimum bandwidth assumption for the 5-second render target? [Clarity, Spec §SC-002] — *Resolved: spec.md now specifies ≥5 Mbps downstream.*

## Requirement Consistency

- [ ] CHK011 - Does FR-003 ("a public Pokémon API") need updating to reflect the clarified choice of PokéAPI documented in Clarifications and Assumptions? [Consistency, Spec §FR-003 / Clarifications] — *Minor inconsistency: FR-003 uses generic wording while Clarifications specify PokéAPI. Resolved in tasks (T014 uses PokéAPI explicitly).*
- [x] CHK012 - Do the "option to retry" in edge cases and the loading/timeout behavior conflict — should retry reset the 15-second timeout or extend it? [Conflict, Spec §Edge Cases] — *No conflict: retry calls loadPokemon() which initiates a fresh request with its own 15s timeout.*

## Acceptance Criteria Quality

- [x] CHK013 - Can SC-003 ("100% of API failure scenarios") be objectively measured given that all possible failure modes are not enumerated in the spec? [Measurability, Spec §SC-003] — *Measurable: tasks (T010) test 404, 500, network error, timeout — all major failure modes.*
- [ ] CHK014 - Is SC-004 ("without experiencing layout shifts") measurable without a defined tolerance or definition of what constitutes a layout shift? [Measurability, Spec §SC-004] — *Partial: tasks (T013, T023) test grid stability but no explicit CLS tolerance defined.*

## Scenario Coverage

- [x] CHK015 - Are requirements defined for the scenario where the API returns successfully but the response contains zero Pokémon? (Edge case exists but no corresponding FR) [Coverage, Spec §Edge Cases] — *Covered: edge case documented, T013 tests empty state.*
- [ ] CHK016 - Are requirements defined for partial load failures — where some Pokémon images load and others fail — beyond the single-image fallback? [Coverage, Gap] — *Implicitly covered by per-card fallback (T011, T015) but not explicitly stated as a scenario.*
- [x] CHK017 - Are requirements defined for the user rapidly navigating back to home and returning to Pokedex (concurrent/rapid navigation)? [Gap] — *Covered: edge case documented, T025 E2E tests navigate-away-back scenario.*

## Edge Case Coverage

- [ ] CHK018 - Are PokéAPI rate limiting scenarios addressed in requirements (e.g., 429 responses, request throttling)? [Gap] — *Gap: not covered.*
- [ ] CHK019 - Are requirements specified for handling Pokémon with very long names that may overflow the card grid layout? [Edge Case, Gap] — *Gap: not covered.*

## Non-Functional Requirements

- [x] CHK020 - Are accessibility requirements specified for the interactive elements (button, grid, load-more trigger), including keyboard navigation and screen reader labeling? [Gap] — *Covered: T026 E2E tests AXE checks, tasks specify role="status" and role="alert".*
- [ ] CHK021 - Are metadata/page-title requirements defined for the Pokedex page (browser tab title, description)? [Gap] — *Gap: not covered.*

## Dependencies & Assumptions

- [x] CHK022 - Is the assumption "the application already has a home page and routing infrastructure" validated, or does it need an explicit dependency declaration? [Assumption, Spec §Assumptions] — *Validated: AGENTS.md confirms presentation/ root files are populated; plan.md says "project already scaffolded."*
- [ ] CHK023 - Is there a fallback or alternative plan documented if PokéAPI becomes unavailable or changes its endpoints? [Assumption, Spec §Assumptions] — *Gap: no fallback plan documented.*

## Notes

- Check items off as completed: `[x]`
- Add comments or findings inline
- Link to relevant resources or documentation
- Items are numbered sequentially (CHK001–CHK023) for easy reference
